summary: The Docker-based PoC successfully demonstrates that an authenticated user can execute arbitrary OS commands through the ExecuteCommand node, confirming complete system compromise capability.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should create a workflow with an ExecuteCommand node containing a malicious command, execute the workflow, and demonstrate command execution by reading /etc/passwd and displaying user identity information.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully created a workflow via the n8n API, executed the malicious command through the ExecuteCommand node, and retrieved output showing successful command execution including /etc/passwd contents and user identity (uid/gid).
verification_method: The verification script checks for four proof signals: (1) the proof string 'EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESSFUL', (2) user execution context, (3) /etc/passwd file disclosure, and (4) successful execution of the 'id' command. All checks must pass for confirmation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Command output includes /etc/passwd file contents (first 5 lines)
Command output shows user identity (uid, gid, groups) from 'id' command
Command output shows current user from 'whoami' command
Workflow was successfully created and executed via authenticated API access

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the n8n repository from GitHub, checks out the latest commit, and mounts it read-only into the container at /target-repo.
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: HEAD (latest commit at time of execution)
